text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously optimistic. The real driver is a hidden Fed upside catalyst story circulating — markets may be underpricing a dovish pivot, which historically rockets tech and chip names. Cybersecurity is leading today per headlines, good for CRWD.

Your holdings: Three need attention. PLTR: down 10.46% and flagged SELL — on margin, this bleed is costing you real borrowed money, no stop reason to wait. AVGO: your largest position, down 14.12% and flagged TRIM — biggest dollar risk in your book, margin amplifies every tick lower. SPCX: brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited price history — size it tiny and watch it daily; margin and new IPOs are a dangerous combo.

Watch: 1) Fed tone — any signal on rate path moves your entire tech/chip book hard. 2) AVGO price action around $336 stop — it's your largest position and most dangerous drawdown.

Bottom line: Cut or trim your two losers (PLTR, AVGO) before the margin math gets worse.
